_description: Real patient p0001 (Arthur Pendleton, MK-0001). 54M, obese, T2DM, asymptomatic with incidental elevated transaminases. R-factor 11.33 — clear hepatitic. Classic NAFLD/MASLD presentation. Expected: no red flag, hepatitic pattern, no dilemma, NAFLD diagnosis, monitoring required.
_expected_path: GP_REFERRAL → EXTRACT → RED_FLAG(no) → TRIAGE → GP_LETTER → LFT_PATTERN(hepatitic) → HEPATITIC_INVESTIGATIONS → DILEMMA(no) → CONSULTATION → EDUCATION → MONITORING(yes) → SURVEILLANCE

symptoms: Evaluation of unexpectedly elevated liver transaminases found during routine insurance medical in a patient with metabolic risk factors, to rule out non-alcoholic fatty liver disease and stage potential fibrosis.
Patient is currently asymptomatic. Denies jaundice, abdominal pain, or fatigue. The issue was found incidentally on routine blood work.
